channel.title: Redbarn Wood Turning Blog
channel.description: Notes on wood and craft from Michael Cedoz's workshop in Mount Vernon, Ohio.

item.title: From Fallen Tree to Finished Bowl: The 18-Month Journey of a Walnut
item.author: michael@redbarnwoodturning.com (Michael Cedoz)
item.description: Follow a single black walnut from a Knox County backyard to a finished bowl. How wood drying, rough turning, and finishing work — and why good bowls take 12 to 18 months.

item.title: Why the Wood Matters More Than You Think
item.author: michael@redbarnwoodturning.com (Michael Cedoz)
item.description: What makes one bowl different from another? The answer starts with the wood. How species, sourcing, and grain shape every piece.

item.title: Anatomy of a Build: The Oak Kitchen Table
item.author: michael@redbarnwoodturning.com (Michael Cedoz)
item.description: How a custom white oak kitchen table comes together — from sourcing slabs to hand-cut joinery to hardwax oil finish.

item.title: How to Care for Your Handmade Wood Piece
item.author: michael@redbarnwoodturning.com (Michael Cedoz)
item.description: Simple care tips for handmade wood furniture, turned bowls, and pens. How to maintain hardwax oil finishes, prevent damage, and keep your piece looking great.

item.title: What Is Wood Turning, Anyway?
item.author: michael@redbarnwoodturning.com (Michael Cedoz)
item.description: An introduction to wood turning — what it is, what it isn't, and why it's not the same as woodworking in general.
